nameMeanings.name: Ramberto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Italian and Spanish: from the personal name Lamberto (see Lambert ).

variants.name: Lambert
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is from an Old French given name of Germanic origin, from land ‘land, territory’ + beorht ‘famous’. This was introduced to Britain by the Normans, but its frequency in Britain in later centuries owed something to immigrants from the Low Countries, and it continued in occasional use into the 18th century at least. St Lambert of Maastricht was a 7th-century saint who aided St Willibrord in his evangelical work.

nameMeanings.name: Ramberti,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Carlo, Gaetano, Luciano, Amedeo, Angelo, Biagio, Domenic, Donato, Enrico, Gino, Iolanda.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Lamberto, an equivalent of Lambert . This surname is also found in Corsica and southern France.
German and Dutch: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  , using the genitive of its Latinized form   Lambertus  .

variants.name: Lambert
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is from an Old French given name of Germanic origin, from land ‘land, territory’ + beorht ‘famous’. This was introduced to Britain by the Normans, but its frequency in Britain in later centuries owed something to immigrants from the Low Countries, and it continued in occasional use into the 18th century at least. St Lambert of Maastricht was a 7th-century saint who aided St Willibrord in his evangelical work.

nameMeanings.name: podestà
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Julio, Armando, Augusto, Gabriela, Guillermo, Horacio, Juan, Justino, Mario, Nestor, Silvia.  Italian Guido, Giacomo, Giovanni, Olympia, Quinto.
Italian (mainly Genoa and Liguria; Podestà): occupational name for a mayor, administrator, or magistrate, podestà.

nameMeanings.name: di
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Diana   and   Diane .

variants.name: Diana
meanings: Name borne in Roman mythology by the goddess of the moon and of hunting, equivalent to the Greek Artemis. In mythology she is characterized as both beautiful and chaste. Her name is of ancient and uncertain derivation. It probably contains a first element that is also found in the name of the Greek god Dionysos ( see Dennis ) and the Latin name of the supreme god Jupiter. It was adopted in Britain during the Tudor period as a learned name, a borrowing from Latin influenced by the French form Diane . Although it was much used by Elizabethan poets celebrating the virgin goddess and alluding to the Virgin Queen, it was not particularly popular as a given name until the end of the 19th century. In earlier centuries some clergymen were reluctant to baptize girls with this pagan name, mindful of the riots against St Paul stirred up by worshippers of Diana of the Ephesians (Acts 19:24–41). In the late 20th century, its popularity received a boost because of its association with the late Diana , Princess of Wales ( 1961–97 ), who was renowned for her beauty, glamour, and compassion.

variants.name: Diane
meanings: form of   Diana  , now also widely used in the English-speaking world. It was especially popular among the Renaissance aristocracy, who loved hunting and were therefore proud to name their daughters after the classical goddess of the chase.

nameMeanings.name: Modena
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Silvio, Angelo, Pasquale, Peppino, Salvatore.
Italian and Jewish (from Italy): habitational name from the city ofModena in Emilia-Romagna.

en.lifeSummary: Ramberto Ramberti, podestà di Modena was born about 1254. He married Margherita di Almerico dei Lavellongo. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died about 1312, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Ramberto Ramberti, podestà di Modena est né environ 1254. Il a épousé Margherita di Almerico dei Lavellongo. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1312, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Ramberto Ramberti, podestà di Modena wurde um 1254 geboren. Er hat Margherita di Almerico dei Lavellongo geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1312 im Alter von 60 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Ramberto Ramberti, podestà di Modena è nato il circa 1254. Lui sposato Margherita di Almerico dei Lavellongo. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì circa 1312, all'età di 60 anni.
